author: judge-reliability-c2

dimensionScores.rationale: Reliability-critical prior findings verified fixed — ONE shared TokenBucket honors NFR-007 (service.py:442), the hard per-fetch watchdog is real and wired (watchdog.py + crawler.py:66, prices.py:69), stooq PoW is bounded (stooq_fetch.py:60-87) — but ~2000 lines of new audited code shipped two SILENT P1s on default/enabled paths: F6 reconcile.py:56,65-66 drops every audited period outside yfinance's ~4yr window for scraped symbols (FSDS deep-history feature inert for ~all US large caps), and F7 companies_house.py:197-198 parses the filing DATE not the company number (UK layer ingests zero rows on real filenames). The fixes offset most of the damage; the two silent, purpose-contradicting P1s + a P2 correctness cluster (F8/F9/F10/F12/F13/F14/F15/F16) hold it just below the prior 3.7.

216 pytest + 62 vitest green, no PerformanceWarning (runs/pytest.txt 3x72 dots, runs/vitest.txt) — broad on the core — but the P1s prove the NEW edge paths are undertested, and worse, F7's own unit fixture (Prod223_1234567.html, single digit-run so digits[-1]==digits[0]) actively MASKS the zero-rows bug while real Prod<nnn>_<batch>_<number>_<date> filenames fail. F6/F13/F14/F16 have no representative fixtures. Green count buys confidence the new audited layer does not earn; docked from prior 3.9 for demonstrated, bug-hiding blind spots.

No new P0/P1 security surface; bounded PoW (F12 fix) removes an untrusted-input CPU-exhaustion vector and the mirror's last-good/atomic contract fails safe. Only new item is F5 (mirror stream has no byte ceiling or total-time cap, mirror.py:80,93) — real but low: URLs are hardcoded/trusted (GLEIF, Frankfurter), no SSRF. Holds at the prior 3.97 band.

Net improvement over prior 3.43: the F4 refactor halved service.py (820->523), the AuditedBulkProvider seam and per-cycle contracts are clean, and mirror.py has a correct temp-then-rename + last-good contract (:92-96,:103-108). Offset by F1 (enrichment.py now the #2 hotspot, 617 LOC / nesting 14, 9 run_* cycles in one module) and F2 (non-atomic meta sidecar). Complexity moved rather than fully dispersed.

Incremental compute (O(dirty) via base.parquet, snapshot.py:245-274) and ETag/304 conditional mirror fetches are solid, deliberate perf wins for the 20k-issuer target; no PerformanceWarning. Docked from prior 4.07 for F11: GLEIF (~1GB decompressed, triple-buffered gleif.py:43-49) and FSDS num.txt (edgar_fsds.py:131) are read whole into RAM on the auto-heal path — an OOM-class regression on a 1GB VPS that defeats the mirror's own streaming design.

topFindings: The three reliability-critical prior findings are genuinely fixed and verified in code (shared TokenBucket service.py:442 honoring NFR-007; hard watchdog call_with_timeout wired at crawler.py:66 and prices.py:69 making the two formerly-lying docstrings true; bounded PoW stooq_fetch.py:60-87) — the CORE crawl/ingest path is materially more reliable than the 76/100 baseline.

F6 (P1, reconcile.py:56,65-66): audited-only periods are silently skipped for any yfinance-scraped symbol, so FSDS/EDGAR deep-history backfill is truncated to the ~4yr scrape window for essentially all US large/mid caps — the Phase-2 FSDS headline feature is inert for its target universe, with no error.

F7 (P1, companies_house.py:197-198): re.findall(r'\d{6,8}') + digits[-1] returns the trailing yyyymmdd DATE, not the company number, on real Accounts Data Product filenames — the entire UK audited layer ingests zero rows and reports 'success'. The unit test fixture is unrepresentative and hides it.

Test-invisibility is the through-line: 216/62 green while the UK layer ingests nothing and US deep history is truncated. Broad green coverage of the core does not extend to the new audited edge paths; one fixture actively masks a P1.

Does not clear the 80 bar (73): core hardening is real, but two silent, test-invisible P1s on default/enabled new audited layers directly contradict stated feature purpose — the new surface does not meet its own expectations. No P0; blast radius partly bounded (EDINET P2s opt-in without a key).
